description: Lock the 2026-06-27 plate_armor correction to European full articulated steel plate harness around 1420, not earlier coats of plates, isolated plate pieces, blast-furnace iron supply, or early modern half-armor.

checks.reason: The date stays anchored to the Met's about-1420 full-plate-harness chronology.
Powered hammering is the source-backed plate-forming method that made plate armor faster and cheaper to produce.
Full plate harness used iron or steel plates with carburizing, case hardening, and related ferrous metallurgy.
Mail remained necessary at joints and gaps that articulated plates could not adequately cover.
Blast furnaces are broader upstream/scaling iron-production context and should not be restored as the direct plate-armor edge without a source narrowing the claim.
